title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 15.4% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, buoyed by their world No. 1 ranking, Euro 2024 title, and unbeaten streak through March friendlies, but France (14.6%), England (11.3%), Argentina (8.9%), and Brazil (8.6%) remain tightly clustered due to comparable squad depth and qualification dominance. The March playoff finals locked in the 48-team field—adding Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Türkiye, and Czechia while excluding Italy—highlighting Europe's parity, with no top contender stumbling. Brazil's odds dipped slightly after Rodrygo's ACL injury, yet their attacking options and South American qualifiers' grind keep them competitive alongside Messi's Argentina and Mbappé's France amid an expanded format favoring upsets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors California Governor Gavin Newsom as the Democratic presidential nominee for 2028 at 27.5% implied probability, driven by his consistent leads in early national polls like Emerson College's recent survey showing 20% support, national fundraising prowess, and executive experience contrasting a fragmented field post-2024 defeat. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.6% on strong progressive grassroots energy and youth appeal, while Georgia Senator Jon Ossoff's 6.7% reflects his swing-state victory and bipartisan profile. Former Vice President Kamala Harris's recent signal of interest in running has not boosted her 5.9% odds amid 2024 baggage. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results, key endorsements, and primary polling in battleground states ahead of the 2027 caucus and primary calendar.

title: The Masters 2026: Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rory McIlroy's historic six-shot lead at 12-under after Rounds 1 (67) and 2 (65)—the largest 36-hole margin in Masters history—anchors his 62% implied probability, fueled by birdie binges on Augusta National's par-5s and precision iron play amid firm, fast conditions. Patrick Reed and Sam Burns lurk at six-under in T2, positioning Reed's 7.2% odds via his 2018 green jacket win and steady ball-striking, while Burns holds 3.3%. Scottie Scheffler's 8.3% reflects two prior victories (2022, 2024) and top-20 Augusta consistency, now T4 at five-under through 10 holes of Round 3. Tommy Fleetwood (4.7%), Cameron Young (3.9%), Justin Rose (3.5%), and Shane Lowry (1.9%) share T4 at five-under, with hot "moving day" rounds needed to challenge amid no major leader injuries.

title: Counter-Strike: Astralis vs FUT Esports (BO5) - PGL Bucharest Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis win the match against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win the match against Astralis.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: Astralis vs FUT Esports - Map 4 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis win Map 4 against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win Map 4 against Astralis.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 4 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 4. 

If Map 4 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Astralis and FUT Esports play 4 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 4 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 4.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Astralis and FUT Esports play 5 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 5 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: AST (-2.5) vs FUT Esports (+2.5)

eventMetadata.context_description: Astralis and FUT Esports clash in the PGL Bucharest 2026 CS2 grand final BO5 after flawless playoff runs, with Astralis edging EYEBALLERS 2-1 in quarters before blanking 3DMAX 2-0 (Dust2 13-11, Overpass 13-10) in semis, while FUT dispatched B8 2-0 and MongolZ 2-0 (Nuke 13-10, Mirage 16-14). Both enter on hot streaks—Astralis with six straight wins, FUT five—bolstering trader views of a razor-close matchup mirrored by their even 6-6 head-to-head record. Astralis (#10 world rank) boasts higher team rating (1.17) and Mirage/Nuke dominance (77%/83% win rates), but FUT's Overpass mastery (86%) and aggression pose upset threats in map vetoes. HooXi's IGL experience versus lauNX's firepower will prove decisive in this $1.25M event's title decider.

title: Military action against Iran ends on...?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next date Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30), on which neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate. 

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action through April 30."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Military action against Iran continues through April 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next date Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30), on which neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate. 

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action through April 30."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends on

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors April 9 (98.6%) as the end date for military action against Iran, driven by a US-Iran ceasefire agreement announced April 8 and holding firm through April 10 amid ongoing Pakistan-mediated talks in Islamabad. No verified airstrikes, missile attacks, or other direct military operations targeting Iranian territory, nuclear sites, petrochemical facilities, or key infrastructure like Kharg Island have occurred since an Israeli airstrike in Beirut on April 8, signaling de-escalation after weeks of US and Israeli strikes that prompted Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure and proxy responses. Iran's submission of four red lines for peace—including Hormuz authority and asset unfreezing—reflects negotiation momentum, with no escalation signals from Tehran or Washington. This commanding position could shift if talks collapse, an Iranian provocation triggers retaliation, or late-breaking intelligence prompts preemptive strikes before the two-week truce expires.

title: Liverpool FC vs. Fulham FC

markets.question: Will Liverpool FC win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If Liverpool FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Liverpool's overwhelming trader consensus at 100% implied probability stems from their title-chasing form in the Premier League, unbeaten Anfield streak this season, and superior squad depth under Arne Slot, with Mohamed Salah, Virgil van Dijk, and Florian Wirtz leading a potent attack despite Alisson's ongoing absence—Giorgi Mamardashvili deputizing solidly. Fulham, mid-table and winless in recent away fixtures against top-six sides, face historical head-to-head struggles, compounded by absences like Tom Cairney and Marek Rodak. Recent 24-hour developments include no new Liverpool injuries pre-kickoff and a strong predicted XI emphasizing home dominance. Realistic challenges include an early red card, Curtis Jones-style in-game injury flare-up, or Fulham's counter threats exploiting transitions.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Péter Magyar at 70.5% implied probability to become Hungary's next prime minister ahead of the April 12 parliamentary election, reflecting polling averages like Politico's Poll of Polls (Tisza 50%, Fidesz 39%) and recent surveys such as Medián's 58-35 Tisza lead among decided voters. Magyar, a former Fidesz insider turned challenger after 2024 scandals, has consolidated opposition support as legacy parties like DK and Momentum withdrew endorsements in his favor. Massive anti-government rallies in Budapest drew tens of thousands in the past week, amplifying discontent with Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule amid economic woes and corruption allegations. Orbán lingers at 29.5% buoyed by Fidesz's incumbency edge in the mixed system—106 first-past-the-post districts and 93 proportional seats—though recent polls diverge, with some government-aligned surveys showing Fidesz ahead. Resolution hinges on post-election government formation.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Direct US-Iran talks commenced on April 11, 2026, in Islamabad, Pakistan—the first high-level diplomatic meeting since 1979—amid a fragile ceasefire following the 2025-2026 war escalation after President Trump's unheeded nuclear deadline. US Vice President JD Vance leads the American delegation alongside envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, facing Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi's team, with host Pakistan mediating on ceasefire terms, Strait of Hormuz access, sanctions relief, and nuclear constraints. Hurdles include mutual ceasefire violation allegations, Israel's Lebanon operations, and Iran's asset release preconditions. Traders eye near-term outcomes, as breakdowns could reignite hostilities or prompt Oman-hosted indirect follow-ups.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Barcelona's virtual certainty as heavy favorites at 100% implied probability in this La Liga Catalan derby stems from their atop-the-table dominance with 76 points and +51 goal difference after 30 matches, fueled by 25 wins and just four losses, while mid-table RCD Espanyol languishes on 38 points amid a winless streak spanning 13 games in 2026 alone. Hosting at Spotify Camp Nou amplifies Barca's edge, backed by overwhelming head-to-head superiority (26 wins in 35 meetings) and recent injury relief for Alejandro Balde and Ronald Araújo despite absences like Raphinha and Andreas Christensen. Traders' skin-in-the-game consensus reflects Espanyol's dismal away record versus Barca, though late scratches, red cards, or derby-fueled counterattacks could spark an improbable upset.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder command trader consensus as 2026 NBA champions after clinching the Western Conference's No. 1 seed and league-best 64-17 record via a seven-game win streak, including a 128-110 rout of the Clippers where Chet Holmgren tallied 30 points and 14 rebounds alongside Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-favorite efficiency. This positions OKC with home-court advantage throughout playoffs starting April 18. San Antonio Spurs trail closely at No. 2 West seed (62-19), surging 29-3 since February and winning sans Victor Wembanyama's recent rib contusion. Boston Celtics hold steady as East No. 2 (55-26), Nuggets ride a 10-game streak to No. 3 West, while East-leading Pistons lag post-Cade Cunningham's 11-game injury return, highlighting West depth preferences.

title: LoL: G2 Esports vs Team Vitality (BO3) - LEC Regular Season
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "G2 Esports" if G2 Esports win the match against Team Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality win the match against G2 Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both G2 Esports and Team Vitality. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:15AM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 2 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game Handicap: VIT (-1.5) vs G2 Esports (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 9:55AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality wins 2 or more games than G2 Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "G2 Esports". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality leads LEC Spring 2026 regular season standings at 4-1 following recent dominant wins, including 2-0 over Team Heretics and 2-1 against Movistar KOI, showcasing strong synergy in their revamped roster with Naak Nako top, Lyncas jungle, Humanoid mid, Carzzy ADC, and Fleshy support. G2 Esports sits fourth at 2-1-1, rebounding with a 2-0 over Heretics but stumbling 1-2 to Fnatic last week amid reported bot lane chemistry issues. G2 holds a commanding head-to-head edge (71% series wins), bolstered by 81% scrim win rate versus Vitality, yet traders price Vit's momentum higher in this BO3 amid Patch 26.6's aggressive meta favoring early jungle duels like Lyncas versus Skewmond.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance commands trader consensus as the slim 2028 presidential frontrunner, buoyed by his April 1 book release—the first by a sitting VP in recent memory—signaling early campaign ambitions, alongside a dominant 53% CPAC 2026 straw poll win and a March UMass Lowell head-to-head poll edge over Gavin Newsom (33%-30%). California Governor Newsom holds close as the top Democrat, propelled by a March California primary poll trouncing Kamala Harris and a February book tour framing his national pitch. This tight race reflects an open-seat contest after President Trump's term limit, nascent primary fields, and pending 2026 midterms; separation could arise from midterm gains in swing states, Trump's endorsement signals, or robust national polling averages.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2028 Republican presidential nomination reflects a tight contest between HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. (49%) and Vice President J.D. Vance (40%), fueled by RFK Jr.'s high-profile swing-state tours alongside GOP lawmakers in battlegrounds like Pennsylvania, Wisconsin, and Michigan to boost midterm turnout, alongside his popular MAHA health reforms that resonate with the base. Vance holds strong as heir apparent, bolstered by topping the CPAC straw poll last week with 53% and his role in ongoing U.S.-Iran peace talks via Pakistan diplomacy. Secretary of State Marco Rubio (22%) gains from donor cheers preferring him over Vance in a recent Trump-hosted meeting. Midterm results and potential Trump endorsements could separate frontrunners in this open primary field.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Sevilla FC at 45.5% implied probability for their La Liga home clash against fourth-placed Club Atlético de Madrid, driven by the visitors' extensive injury doubts—José Giménez, Johnny Cardoso, Pablo Barrios, Dávid Hancko—and suspensions to Koke and Nico González, forcing heavy rotations ahead of their Champions League quarter-final second leg versus Barcelona following a 2-0 first-leg win. Diego Simeone's confirmed lineup features backup goalkeeper Juan Musso and youth like Javier Boñar and Rayane Belaid, with stars Antoine Griezmann and Julián Álvarez on the bench. Relegation-threatened Sevilla (17th, 31 points), despite three straight La Liga losses and the second-worst home record, gain from desperation and Ramón Sánchez-Pizjuán advantage, tempering Atlético's recent head-to-head dominance amid their own back-to-back league defeats. The tight 30.5% for Atlético and 26.5% draw odds underscore the matchup's competitiveness.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket trader consensus prices a 98.3% implied probability for no change in the Federal Reserve's federal funds rate target range of 3.50%-3.75% at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reinforced by the March 17-18 decision to hold steady amid resilient economic conditions. March nonfarm payrolls rose 178,000 with unemployment steady near 4.4%, while recent CPI data showed persistent inflation above the 2% target, curbing cut expectations despite oil price volatility. FOMC minutes released April 8 highlighted officials' openness to hikes if pressures intensify, aligning market-implied path with dot-plot forecasts for just one 25 basis point reduction later in 2026. Upside inflation surprises in mid-April CPI or weakening labor indicators could challenge this positioning ahead of the meeting.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Brentford FC vs. Everton FC Premier League match at Gtech Community Stadium ended in a 2-2 draw, locking in trader consensus at 100% on the Draw outcome as the official result after 90 minutes plus stoppage time. Igor Thiago's early penalty and 76th-minute strike gave Brentford a two-goal lead, but Everton responded with Beto's 26th-minute equalizer and a dramatic stoppage-time finish from Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall, earning a vital point in their shared push for European spots—Brentford sit 7th, Everton 8th in the table. This result avenges Everton's earlier 2-4 home loss to Brentford in January, reflecting both teams' solid recent form with few clean sheets in tight head-to-heads. No realistic challenges remain post-resolution.

title: LoL: Top Esports vs JD Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Top Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:00AM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TES (-1.5) vs JD Gaming (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:20AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports wins 2 or more games than JD Gaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "JD Gaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Top Esports holds a strong 3-1 record in LPL Split 2 Group Ascend double round-robin, riding a three-win streak including 2-0 sweeps over Anyone's Legend and Team WE, positioning them second behind Invictus Gaming ahead of this pivotal BO3 matchup under Fearless Draft rules. JD Gaming sits at 1-2 after a 1-2 loss to Bilibili Gaming but rebounded with a 2-0 win over Anyone's Legend yesterday, highlighting top laner Xiaoxu's dominance amid patch 26.7 meta shifts favoring aggressive engages. TES boasts a slight historical BO3 edge (16-13 series wins), though JDG upset them 3-2 in Split 1 playoffs; top-four finish here secures direct playoff qualification, amplifying stakes in this evenly matched Group Ascend clash with full rosters confirmed.

title: LoL: Team WE vs Invictus Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Invictus Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Team WE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Team WE and Invictus Gaming. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 1, or if Game 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Invictus Gaming tops LPL Split 2 Group Ascend standings at 3-0 series with a 6-1 game record, driving strong trader consensus as favorites against struggling Team WE, who have just 1 win in their last 5 series following a mixed Split 1 finish. IG's recent dominance stems from sharp macro execution and new head coach Helper's integration since late March, highlighted by efficient early-game leads and objective secures in wins over Top Esports and others. WE faces consistency issues in picks/bans and mid-game fights, despite even historical head-to-head (15-16 series each). No roster changes or player absences reported ahead of this BO3 group stage clash, with current patch favoring IG's aggressive style.

title: Indian Premier League: Chennai Super Kings vs Delhi Capitals
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Chennai Super Kings and Delhi Capitals scheduled for April 11 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Chennai Super Kings vs Delhi Capitals - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Chennai Super Kings and Delhi Capitals scheduled for 2026-04-11 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Chennai Super Kings vs Delhi Capitals - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Chennai Super Kings and Delhi Capitals scheduled for 2026-04-11 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chennai Super Kings enter this Chepauk clash on a winless 0-3 skid with a dismal -2.517 NRR, hampered by MS Dhoni's ongoing calf injury rehab—ruling him out—and a leaky bowling unit exposed in recent thrashings, including a 43-run loss to RCB. Dewald Brevis's return from side strain bolsters their batting alongside Ruturaj Gaikwad and new recruit Sanju Samson, who seeks form on home soil. Delhi Capitals, buoyed by two chase wins in three (over LSG and MI) despite a narrow 1-run defeat to GT, hold mid-table momentum with Axar Patel's all-round threat. CSK lead head-to-head 19-12, but a flatter, high-scoring pitch favors batsmen amid clear weather, heightening upset potential in this IPL encounter.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' overwhelming dominance in the early 2026 F1 season, powered by superior power units under new regulations, positions George Russell and teammate Kimi Antonelli as overwhelming trader favorites for the Drivers' Championship, capturing over 73% implied probability combined. Antonelli leads standings with 72 points after back-to-back victories in China and Japan—claiming pole over Russell in Suzuka—trailing by just nine from Russell's consistent podiums, including his Australian Grand Prix win. Despite the rookie's hot streak, traders favor the experienced Russell at 43.5% for his racecraft and lower error risk over a 24-race grind, while Ferrari's Charles Leclerc lags at third with 49 points amid Red Bull and McLaren's struggles, keeping challengers like Verstappen and Norris under 3%.

title: # of views of next MrBeast video on week 1?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of views the next YouTube video posted by MrBeast after this market's creation gets in the first 7 days after being posted.

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders have locked in near-unanimous consensus on 80-90 million first-week YouTube views for MrBeast's latest blockbuster, "50 Streamers Fight for $1,000,000," uploaded April 4, 2026, which hit exactly 80.9 million views by April 11 after explosive growth from 33.9 million on day one and 51 million by day two. This positioning reflects Jimmy Donaldson's unmatched subscriber army exceeding 400 million, high-stakes streamer collaborations driving viral buzz—including a live final-four showdown that peaked at over one million concurrent viewers—and his proven track record of 80-100 million week-one hauls on recent challenges like "Survive 30 Days Stranded With Your Ex." With week one concluded and views firmly in range, upset scenarios are negligible barring rare YouTube recount disputes, underscoring prediction markets' wisdom-of-crowds efficiency in tracking creator metrics.

title: Burnley FC vs. Brighton & Hove Albion FC
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between Burnley FC and Brighton & Hove Albion FC.

markets.question: Will Burnley FC win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If Burnley FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Brighton's 2-0 victory at Turf Moor, sealed by Mats Wieffer's brace including a late edge-of-box strike, has driven trader consensus to a 100% implied probability on the Seagulls' win, reflecting the official Premier League result. Burnley, second-bottom and 12 points from safety with six games left, remain winless in their last six league matches and have just one victory since October, hampered by two VAR-disallowed goals for tight offsides from Jaidon Anthony and Bashir Humphreys. Brighton, ninth and chasing Europa League spots on a three-win streak, dominated possession (51%) and shots (13-10) against a relegation-threatened Clarets side they've beaten in three of the last 10 head-to-heads. Overturn scenarios are negligible absent rare administrative protests or league revisions.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Datafolha polling from early April shows Senator Flávio Bolsonaro edging President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva 46%-45% in a simulated runoff for Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential election, a statistical tie reflecting voter polarization. Ideia and Paraná Pesquisas surveys from the same period confirm near-ties in first-round scenarios, with Lula at 40-41% and Flávio at 37-38%, narrowing from Lula's wider leads earlier this year. Traders price this closely contested race—Lula 39.5%, Flávio 39.4%—due to Flávio consolidating right-wing support via his father's endorsement amid Lula's 53% rejection rate, fuel crisis subsidies, and corruption scandals. A likely runoff awaits; economic recovery, campaign endorsements, or regional vote shifts could tip the balance.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 9 - April 11, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 9 12:00 PM ET to April 11, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly backs the 65-89 tweet range for Elon Musk's X activity from April 9-11, 2026, with implied probabilities at 100%, driven by real-time trackers like XTracker confirming cumulative posts aligning precisely in this bin as of April 11 afternoon (last synced 2:30 PM EST). Elon's steady posting rhythm—averaging around 22-25 per day amid commentary on SpaceX splashdowns, political memes, and AI developments—has shown no spikes in the past 48 hours, solidifying the frontrunner amid his typical evening cadence. Historical patterns from recent multi-day markets support this positioning, as volumes rarely exceed 30 daily without major catalysts. Realistic upsets would require a late-breaking event, like a Tesla earnings surprise or viral controversy, triggering a 20+ post frenzy before midnight ET cutoff.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Keiko Fujimori as the frontrunner to win Peru's presidential election amid a fragmented first-round field of over 35 candidates voting April 12, with a likely June 7 runoff, driven by her consistent lead in final publishable Ipsos, Datum, and CPI polls from early April showing 16-18% support post-debates, fueled by voter anger over soaring crime rates and corruption scandals that have toppled multiple presidents since 2016. Ricardo Belmont's competitive odds stem from rising buzz in private IEP surveys placing him near Fujimori and Rafael López Aliaga, leveraging his media profile among undecided voters (17-27% in polls). The tight race persists due to high volatility, regional vote splits, and no candidate nearing 50%; separation could arise from turnout in Lima battlegrounds, right-wing consolidation, or last-minute endorsements before polls close.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 10 - April 17, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 10 12:00 PM ET to April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns top implied probabilities to 240-259 posts (27.5%) and nearby ranges through 220-279, mirroring Elon Musk's recent X posting pace of 30-35 per day, as evidenced by 99 posts over the first three days of the overlapping April 7-14 market (33/day average) and daily highs like 46 on April 7 amid peak platform engagement records. Closely matched frontrunners reflect the competitive dynamics of Musk's variable output—steady baselines punctuated by spikes from Tesla updates, SpaceX milestones, or viral political commentary—creating uncertainty in this 8-day window. Key swing factors include any breaking news catalysts before April 17, though his cultural dominance on X sustains consistent high-volume engagement without recent disruptions.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile two-week ceasefire between Iran, the United States, and Israel, brokered by Pakistan on April 7-8, has held without direct exchanges of fire since April 8, driving trader consensus toward potential de-escalation despite ongoing Israeli airstrikes against Hezbollah in Lebanon and southern Lebanon, which Iran insists must be included in the truce framework. Direct US-Iran negotiations began April 11 in Islamabad, led by US Vice President JD Vance and Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf, focusing on Iran's nuclear program, sanctions relief, Strait of Hormuz access, and regional ceasefires amid Gulf drone attacks. The truce expires around April 22, with first-round talks concluding as delegations exchange texts; breakdown risks from proxy escalations or stalled diplomacy could prolong hostilities.

title: Capitals vs. Penguins
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 11 at 3:00PM ET:
If the Capitals win, the market will resolve to "Capitals".
If the Penguins win, the market will resolve to "Penguins".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pittsburgh Penguins host Washington Capitals at PPG Paints Arena in their final regular-season home game, with Penguins resting stars Sidney Crosby (lower-body), Evgeni Malkin (upper-body), Bryan Rust (lower-body), Erik Karlsson (lower-body), Kris Letang (upper-body), and others as day-to-day injuries to prioritize playoff health, enabling emergency recalls of Ville Koivunen, Rutger McGroarty, and Joona Koppanen. Washington backup goaltender Charlie Lindgren (upper body) practiced fully yesterday but remains day-to-day, with Logan Thompson or John Gibson likely in net alongside Alex Ovechkin chasing goals on the top line. Both Metro rivals pace 93-101 points with Penguins holding superior goal differential (+25 vs. +15), power play edge (24.7% vs. 17.4%), yet Pittsburgh's absences tilt trader consensus toward Capitals in this pivotal late-season clash.
